title: unwrap/expect panics in production paths
description:
Several .unwrap()/.expect() calls exist in non-test code where the panic can reach production:

- db.rs:71 — SqliteConnectOptions::from_str(db_path.to_str().unwrap()) panics on non-UTF-8 path
- cli.rs:120 — path.to_str().unwrap() in Init command, same issue
- delivery.rs:96 — .expect("building HTTP client") inside the background worker; a panic here kills the delivery loop silently (tokio task just ends)
- delivery.rs:268 — chrono::Duration::from_std(delay).unwrap(); Duration::from_std returns Err on overflow, which is reachable if RETRY_DELAYS ever gets a very large value
- server.rs:477 — serde_json::to_vec(&accept).unwrap_or_default() sends an empty body Accept if serialization fails, which is protocol-incorrect

What goes wrong: db.rs/cli.rs panics crash the process on non-UTF-8 data directories (a real case on some Linux setups). The delivery.rs worker panic is swallowed by tokio and the queue silently stops draining — no error log, no restart. The server.rs case sends a malformed Accept to the follower's server.

Fix: db.rs and cli.rs — use Path::to_str().ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("path is not UTF-8"))?. delivery.rs worker — use .context() and propagate, or handle the error explicitly. server.rs — propagate serialization error into the if let block's error arm.
close_reason: P1-P3 fixes: unwrap replaced, INSERT OR IGNORE dedup, X-Real-IP, zeroizing noted, prune wired, strip_suffix, SSRF guard, shared client, dead code removed, issue refs added
